controls.note: Identity managed via Microsoft Entra ID. Quarterly access reviews completed. WARNING: device authorization incomplete for manufacturing floor — see objective c.

objectiveNotes.c: Legacy endpoints on manufacturing floor not yet enrolled in Intune. Remediation ticket ITSM-4412 opened. Policy exception in place for subnet.

a.interviews: Interviewed IT administrator. User access reviews conducted quarterly via Entra ID Access Reviews. All accounts tied to active HR records.
a.examine: Reviewed Entra ID user export dated 2026-05-15. 47 licensed users identified, each with documented business justification and role assignment.
a.test: Verified provisioning workflow — new hire account creation requires manager approval in ServiceNow before Entra ID provisioning completes.
a.overallComments: User identification controls are solid. Quarterly review cadence is documented and recurring.

c.interviews: Interviewed network admin. BYOD and legacy endpoints on manufacturing floor not enrolled in Intune.
c.examine: Reviewed Intune device compliance report. 12 legacy devices flagged as non-compliant.
c.test: Confirmed policy exception allows manufacturing floor subnet to bypass device compliance check.
c.overallComments: NOT MET — remediation in progress. Target close date 2026-07-01.

controls.note: Role-based access enforced via Entra ID groups and conditional access. Privilege separation documented.

a.interviews: Confirmed role separation with IT admin — RBAC enforced through Entra ID security groups.
a.examine: Reviewed group membership and access matrix. Roles align to job functions.

controls.note: CUI flow controls partially implemented. Data classification labels in M365 deployed. DLP policies under review.

objectiveNotes.b: DLP policy scope needs expansion to cover SharePoint external sharing.

controls.note: Separation of duties policy drafted. Implementation in progress for financial and admin roles.
Least privilege enforced via Entra ID PIM. Privileged roles require JIT activation with business justification.

a.interviews: Confirmed PIM activation workflow with security team. All privileged role activations require approval.
a.examine: Reviewed PIM audit log — no standing privileged access found for non-emergency accounts.
a.test: Tested JIT activation flow for Global Admin role — approval required and logged.
a.overallComments: Least privilege posture is well-implemented through Entra PIM.

controls.note: Non-privileged accounts used for day-to-day tasks. Admin accounts separate from standard accounts per policy.
Privileged function audit logging enabled in Sentinel. Review of privileged activity reports ongoing.

objectiveNotes.c: Automated alerting on privileged role changes not yet configured.

controls.note: Account lockout policy configured in Entra ID. Smart lockout enabled with threshold of 10 failed attempts.
Screen lock policy deployed via Intune for managed devices. Coverage gaps for legacy workstations.
Session timeout configured for web apps. Desktop session termination policy not yet enforced uniformly.
Remote access via GlobalProtect VPN with Entra ID SSO. Split tunneling disabled for CUI-scoped traffic.
Remote access sessions encrypted via TLS 1.2+. GlobalProtect enforces encryption for all tunneled traffic.
Remote access routing through managed gateways partially implemented. Non-managed device path still under review.
Privileged remote access controls incomplete. Admin sessions not yet isolated to dedicated jump hosts.

objectiveNotes.a: Jump server procurement approved. Deployment scheduled Q3 2026.

controls.note: Wireless access points inventoried. WPA3-Enterprise configured on primary SSID. Guest network segmentation partially complete.
Mobile device connections managed via Microsoft Intune MAM/MDM. Only compliant devices permitted to access CUI.
CUI on mobile devices encrypted at rest via Intune app protection policies. BitLocker enforced on Windows endpoints.
External connections limited via firewall policy. Deny-by-default posture enforced at perimeter.
Portable storage use not yet restricted. USB policy drafted but enforcement via Intune DLP not deployed.

objectiveNotes.a: Intune USB restriction policy in test environment. Production deployment pending change approval.

controls.note: Public-facing website reviewed. CUI scrub in progress — two pages identified with potential CUI references under review.
Security awareness training deployed via M365 Defender. Completion rate at 78%. Role-based tracks for IT and finance in development.
Role-based training tracks drafted. IT admin track completed. Finance and operations tracks pending content review.
Audit logging enabled across all in-scope systems. Logs forwarded to Microsoft Sentinel. 12-month retention configured.

a.interviews: Confirmed with SOC lead that Sentinel ingests logs from all in-scope systems.
a.examine: Reviewed Sentinel workspace data sources. 14 connectors active including Entra ID, Defender, and network appliances.
a.test: Generated test event and confirmed log appeared in Sentinel within 2 minutes.
a.overallComments: Logging coverage is comprehensive. Retention verified at 12 months.

controls.note: User accountability enforced through individual accounts and Sentinel audit trails. No shared accounts in use.
Audit log review process documented. Weekly review scheduled but not yet consistently executed.

objectiveNotes.c: Automated review workflow not yet implemented — manual process only.

controls.note: Audit failure alerts configured in Sentinel. Alerts routed to SOC email and Teams channel.
Audit correlation rules active in Sentinel. Cross-source correlation for lateral movement and privilege escalation scenarios in development.
Time synchronization enforced via NTP hierarchy. All domain members sync to authoritative internal time source.
Audit log protection configured in Sentinel — logs immutable once written. Log integrity alerting not yet implemented.

Baseline configurations documented for servers and workstations. Assessment Status set NOT MET pending formal baseline approval — all objectives are factually satisfied. See status consistency warning.

objectiveNotes.a: Baseline documentation complete. Awaiting CISO sign-off.

controls.note: Security configuration baselines applied via Intune configuration profiles. Coverage incomplete for Linux servers.
Change management process documented in ITSM. Security impact analysis not consistently performed for low-priority changes.

objectiveNotes.b: Change review board established but security review step is optional for minor changes.

controls.note: Access restrictions for configuration changes not yet enforced. Any domain admin can modify GPO without change record.

objectiveNotes.a: GPO modification rights need to be scoped to configuration management team only.

controls.note: Least functionality review in progress. Application whitelist being built via Intune app management.
Application execution policy not yet implemented. AppLocker/WDAC evaluation underway.

objectiveNotes.a: WDAC pilot deployment on 5 workstations in progress.
objectiveNotes.b: Policy exceptions documented but enforcement not yet active.

controls.note: User-installed software policy exists but is not enforced. Users can install software without IT approval.
User and device identification fully implemented via Entra ID. Assessment Status set NOT MET pending formal documentation approval — all objectives satisfied. See status consistency warning.

a.interviews: Confirmed with IT admin that all system users are provisioned via Entra ID with unique identifiers.
a.examine: Reviewed Entra ID user export. All accounts have unique UPN and are tied to HR records.
a.test: Verified no shared or generic accounts in active directory.
a.overallComments: User identification is fully implemented. Documentation approval pending.

controls.note: Authentication enforced via Entra ID. Password policies and MFA requirements documented and active.
MFA enforced for all users accessing CUI systems via Entra ID conditional access. FIDO2 keys deployed for privileged accounts.

a.interviews: Confirmed with security engineer — all Entra ID accounts enrolled in MFA. No MFA bypass exceptions active.
a.examine: Reviewed conditional access policy — MFA required for all cloud app sign-ins for all users.
a.test: Tested MFA enforcement by signing in from new device — MFA prompt triggered successfully.
a.overallComments: MFA implementation is strong. FIDO2 for privileged accounts adds additional assurance.

controls.note: Replay-resistant authentication enforced via Entra ID — OAuth 2.0 tokens with short expiry and PKCE flow.
Identifier reuse controls documented. Retired account UPNs not reused — policy in place but automated enforcement pending.
Identifier lifecycle management process documented. Disabled accounts purged after 90 days per Entra ID retention policy.
Password complexity enforced via Entra ID password protection. Minimum 12 characters, complexity requirements active.
Password reuse restricted — Entra ID blocks last 24 passwords. Policy documented and enforced.
Temporary password process documented. Forced change on first login enforced for new accounts. Admin reset flow under review.
Passwords stored as hashed credentials in Entra ID. No plaintext password storage permitted.
Password feedback obscured during entry on all in-scope systems. Verified on web apps and Windows login screens.
Incident response plan drafted and approved. Tabletop exercise completed Q1 2026. Containment runbooks in development.

objectiveNotes.d: Eradication runbooks for ransomware scenario not yet finalized.

controls.note: Incident reporting procedures documented. DIBCAC reporting path identified. Internal escalation matrix finalized.
Maintenance activities logged in ITSM. Authorized maintenance personnel list maintained. Veeam used for backup-level recovery testing.
Remote maintenance controls partially implemented. Out-of-band access via jump server in planning.

objectiveNotes.b: Remote maintenance sessions not yet isolated to dedicated channel.

controls.note: Nonlocal maintenance sessions not authenticated using MFA. Standard credentials used for remote admin sessions.

objectiveNotes.a: MFA for maintenance sessions requires PAM solution. Currently in procurement.
objectiveNotes.b: Session recording not yet in place.

controls.note: Maintenance personnel vetting process in place for internal staff. Third-party vendor controls under review.
Media disposal procedures documented and implemented. Hard drives degaussed or shredded. Certificate of destruction on file.
Media protection policy drafted. Physical media inventory in progress. Digital media controls via Intune partially deployed.
Access to CUI media restricted to authorized personnel. Physical storage room access controlled by badge reader.
Removable media accountability log in place. Chain of custody process for external drives documented.
Portable storage containing CUI encrypted using BitLocker. Intune policy enforces encryption before data can be written.
Removable media use not restricted. USB ports active on workstations. Intune restriction policy not yet deployed to production.
Shared media sanitization procedures not documented. No formal process for sanitizing shared drives before reuse.
Backups protected via Veeam Backup & Replication with immutable backup repository. Restoration test performed Q1 2026.

a.interviews: Confirmed with backup administrator — daily full backups to immutable Azure Blob storage. 30-day retention.
a.examine: Reviewed Veeam backup job report — all jobs completing successfully. No failed jobs in past 90 days.
a.test: Observed restoration test on 2026-03-14. Single VM restored successfully from 7-day-old backup.
a.overallComments: Backup protection posture is strong. Immutability and tested recovery meet the requirement.

controls.note: Background checks completed for all personnel with access to CUI. Screening conducted by HR prior to access provisioning.
Personnel termination process documented. Access revocation occurs within 4 hours of HR notification. Formal offboarding checklist used.

a.interviews: Confirmed with HR — termination tickets trigger automatic Entra ID account disable via automated workflow.
a.examine: Reviewed 5 termination tickets. All accounts disabled within 2 hours of HR initiation.
a.test: Verified disabled account cannot authenticate via Entra ID sign-in logs.
a.overallComments: Termination process is well-controlled and automated. Offboarding checklist includes badge return and device recovery.

controls.note: Physical access to facility limited to authorized personnel. Badge reader access logs maintained. Visitor escort policy in place.
Visitor escort policy enforced. Visitors signed in at reception and escorted at all times in restricted areas.
Physical access logs maintained via badge reader system. Logs retained for 12 months and reviewed monthly.
Physical access reviews conducted quarterly. Access rights for terminated employees revoked. Access for contractors under review.

objectiveNotes.b: Contractor access review not yet completed for Q2 2026.

controls.note: CCTV coverage of facility entrance and server room deployed. Camera coverage gaps in loading dock area.

objectiveNotes.d: Loading dock camera installation scheduled for July 2026.

controls.note: Risk assessment completed in Q4 2025. Tenable.sc used for vulnerability discovery. Next scheduled assessment Q3 2026.

objectiveNotes.e: Risk register not yet formally approved by senior leadership.

controls.note: Vulnerability scanning running weekly via Tenable.sc. Credentialed scans cover servers. Workstation scanning coverage at 85%.

objectiveNotes.d: Remediation SLAs defined but not yet consistently tracked in vulnerability tracker.

a.interviews: Confirmed with security analyst — Tenable.sc scans run every Sunday at 2am against all in-scope subnets.
a.examine: Reviewed Tenable scan report dated 2026-06-01. 3 critical vulnerabilities identified across 2 servers.
a.test: Verified credentialed scan results show patch-level detail for Windows servers.
a.overallComments: Scanning cadence is appropriate. Remediation tracking needs improvement.

controls.note: Vulnerability remediation process documented. SLAs: Critical 30 days, High 60 days, Medium 90 days. Tracking via vulnerability-remediation-tracker.

objectiveNotes.a: 3 critical vulnerabilities from latest scan past 30-day SLA — escalation in progress.

controls.note: Security control assessment scope defined. Assessment activities in progress — 60% of controls evaluated.
POA&M established. 8 open items tracked. Items prioritized by risk score. Review cadence is monthly.
System Security Plan in development. Boundary and data flow diagrams complete. Control implementation descriptions 70% complete.

objectiveNotes.g: Third-party service provider list needs to be finalized in SSP.

controls.note: Network boundary protection implemented via Palo Alto Networks NGFW. Deny-by-default ruleset with explicit allow entries only.

a.interviews: Confirmed with network engineer — Palo Alto NGFW deployed at all network boundaries. IPS signatures updated weekly.
a.examine: Reviewed firewall ruleset. Default-deny policy confirmed. All allow rules have documented business justification.
a.test: Attempted connection to blocked port from DMZ — connection refused as expected.
a.overallComments: Boundary protection is well-implemented. Palo Alto fully inherited for this control.

controls.note: Public-access systems separated from internal CUI-handling systems via DMZ architecture. Palo Alto enforces separation.
Security engineering principles applied to new system designs. Legacy system review for security architecture alignment in progress.
Network communications deny-by-default. Only explicitly approved connections permitted through Palo Alto ruleset.
Split tunneling disabled on GlobalProtect VPN. All remote traffic routes through corporate gateway.
CUI in transit encrypted via TLS 1.2 minimum. Full inheritance from M365 GCC High. Inheritance source intentionally left blank — pending documentation update.
Session termination after inactivity configured for web apps. Network session termination controls not yet uniformly applied.
Key management policy drafted but no formal key management system in place. Encryption keys managed ad hoc.

objectiveNotes.a: Key management solution (Azure Key Vault) approved for procurement.
objectiveNotes.b: Key rotation schedule not yet established.

controls.note: CUI encryption at rest partially implemented. M365 GCC High encrypts data at rest natively. On-premise storage encryption review pending.

objectiveNotes.a: On-premise file server encryption using BitLocker not yet verified for all volumes.

controls.note: Mobile code controls not implemented. JavaScript and other mobile code not restricted or monitored in browser sessions.
Communications authenticity controls not in place. Email signing (S/MIME or DKIM) not enforced for internal communications.

objectiveNotes.a: DKIM configured for outbound email but not for internal. S/MIME deployment planned.

controls.note: CUI at rest encrypted in M365 GCC High using Microsoft-managed keys. SharePoint and Exchange Online both covered.

Flaw remediation managed via Tenable.sc and patch management procedure. CrowdStrike provides real-time vulnerability telemetry. WARNING: objective d (patch application timeliness) NOT MET due to overdue critical patches.

objectiveNotes.d: 3 critical patches for Windows Server overdue past 30-day SLA. Remediation ticket ITSM-5501 open.

d.interviews: Confirmed with patch admin — 3 Windows Server patches delayed due to change freeze for Q2 audit.
d.examine: Reviewed Tenable remediation tracker. CVE-2025-21298, CVE-2025-24054, CVE-2025-26647 past SLA.
d.overallComments: Patch application SLA breached for 3 critical CVEs. Change freeze lifted 2026-06-01 — patches being applied.

controls.note: Malicious code protection via CrowdStrike Falcon deployed on all endpoints and servers.
Security alerts and advisories from CISA and vendor bulletins monitored. CrowdStrike threat intelligence integrated.
CrowdStrike Falcon definitions updated automatically. Definition age never exceeds 24 hours across the fleet.
System and file scanning performed continuously via CrowdStrike Falcon real-time protection.
Network communications monitoring via Sentinel and Palo Alto threat prevention. Coverage for east-west traffic incomplete.

objectiveNotes.b: Internal network traffic monitoring (east-west) not yet enabled.

controls.note: Unauthorized use detection via Sentinel UEBA. Baseline behavioral profiles under development. Alert tuning ongoing.
